pr.title: Move .data section to respective files up to the Target Test stages
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Large mechanical cleanup that removed most data from the monolithic asm/data.s and relocated .data blocks into their owning assembly source files, especially stage files under asm/melee/gr and a large block in asm/melee/text_2.s. It also moved several .bss symbols out of asm/bss.s into asm/melee/mp/code_80041C78.s, deleted the temporary FORCEFILES block from ldscript.lcf, and normalized grkraid_ to grkraid in obj_files.mk. No PR body or review comments were present in the slice, so intent beyond the title and diff is inferred from the file movement.
what_changed: Deleted 14287 lines from asm/data.s, leaving the former central data file essentially emptied of the moved symbols.

Appended .section .data blocks to many owning assembly files, with the largest recipients including asm/melee/text_2.s, asm/melee/gr/grpura.s, asm/melee/gr/grmutecity.s, asm/melee/gr/grcorneria.s, asm/melee/gr/gricemt.s, and asm/melee/gr/grbigblue.s.

Moved stage-related data tables, string literals, function pointer tables, NULL entries, .asciz strings, and .balign directives into the corresponding asm/melee/gr/*.s files instead of keeping them in asm/data.s.
Removed four .bss labels from asm/bss.s: lbl_80458810, lbl_80458868, lbl_80458888, and lbl_80458E88.
Added those moved .bss labels to asm/melee/mp/code_80041C78.s under `.section .bss, "wa"`.
Removed the temporary FORCEFILES block from ldscript.lcf, including many stage object entries and runtime support objects that had been forced during the transitional layout.
Updated obj_files.mk to use `asm/melee/gr/grkraid.o` instead of `asm/melee/gr/grkraid_.o`, matching the diff rename from grkraid_.s to grkraid.s.
smart_moves: Kept symbol labels and raw data directives intact while relocating them, which is the safest way to preserve binary layout during a pure ownership cleanup.

Co-located data with the assembly file that references or owns it, making later decompilation easier because local tables and strings are near their code rather than hidden in one global asm/data.s blob.
Removed linker-script FORCEFILES after data had been distributed to real object files, reducing temporary linker scaffolding and making object ordering more explicit through normal build inputs.
Handled .bss separately from .data by moving the relevant skip-reserved symbols into the owning mp assembly file with the correct writable-alloc section flags.
Normalized the grkraid file/object naming by dropping the temporary underscore suffix, reducing a source/object name mismatch.
decomp_lessons: For early decomp cleanup, relocating unknown data to the likely owning asm file is valuable even before replacing it with C definitions; it narrows future search space and clarifies object boundaries.
When splitting a monolithic data file, preserve labels, order, alignment directives, string encodings, and NULL placeholders exactly; the value is organizational, not semantic rewriting.
Once data is in its owning object, temporary linker FORCEFILES entries may become unnecessary and should be removed to avoid stale build-order hacks.
BSS migration should preserve section attributes and skip sizes exactly; changing only location while keeping `.section .bss, "wa"` and `.skip` sizes avoids accidental layout drift.
Object-list naming should match source filenames; temporary suffixes like grkraid_ are a maintenance hazard once the source can be named normally.
naming_conventions: Stage assembly files use lowercase `gr` prefixes under `asm/melee/gr/`, such as grbattle.s, grbigblue.s, grcorneria.s, grmutecity.s, and grpura.s.

The PR changed the temporary `grkraid_` naming to `grkraid` in obj_files.mk and the source path, suggesting a preference for object names matching the stage module name without an underscore workaround.
Unidentified symbols remained address-style labels such as lbl_803E7DA0 and lbl_80458810; the PR did not attempt semantic renaming.
Assembly section declarations in relocated files used explicit `.section .data` for initialized data and `.section .bss, "wa"` for zero-initialized storage.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Bulk data movement was done by appending `.section .data` blocks to existing assembly translation units after their code, preserving the raw directives from asm/data.s.
Relocated data retained `.4byte`, `.asciz`, `.balign`, function-pointer references, label-pointer references, and NULL entries rather than converting them to C declarations.
The linker script cleanup indicates the relocated data/object layout allowed removal of a temporary FORCEFILES workaround.
The diff evidence supports a layout/ownership cleanup rather than a function-matching improvement; no new C functions or matching tricks are shown.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or review records were present in the provided PR slice.
No explicit reviewer rationale or requested changes were available; conclusions are based on the title, changed-file list, and diff excerpt.
follow_up_queries: Which later PRs convert the relocated asm/melee/gr/*.s .data blocks into C structs or named arrays?
When was asm/data.s fully eliminated or reduced further after PR 166?
Did the grkraid_ to grkraid rename require any build-system follow-up in later PRs?
Which PRs remove the remaining lbl_80458EB0 and later bss entries from asm/bss.s?
How did later Target Test stage decompilation use the data placement established by this cleanup?
